titleStmt.title: Letter from Major Luscombe to Sir Matthew Nathan, 30 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Major George A. Luscombe

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Major George A. Luscombe (c.1864-?) to Sir Matthew Nathan, Under Secretary for Ireland. Nathan (1862-1939) was the Under-Secretary for Ireland from 1914 until 1916. In 1916 he faced criticism for failing to be aware of and to prevent the outbreak of the Easter Rising on 24 April.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3906

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle. Sir, I beg to submit the attached
application for exemption from
internment on behalf Adolf
Ether, a prisoner of war in this
camp. I have the honour,
To be Sir,
Your obt Serv M.T. Luscombe
Major
Commandant Chief Com
please send
usual form
EOF
31.1.16 Supt. B.dmn
W.A.M. 1/2/16. Inspt Willoughley
For report
1/2/16
J Dunne
Chief Supt
